entities.name: HIGH THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING
entities.description: An emerging field aiming to bring rapid, non-invasive sensing technology to agriculture to accelerate the estimation of plant traits significantly

entities.name: COMPUTER VISION-BASED AUTOMATED 3D POINT CLOUD PROCESSING FRAMEWORK
entities.description: A framework for accurate estimation of essential phenotypic traits involving sub-plot detection, crop extraction, and trait estimation

entities.name: SUB-PLOT DETECTION
entities.description: The first step in the point cloud processing framework to identify sub-plots

entities.name: CROP EXTRACTION
entities.description: The second step in the point cloud processing framework to isolate the crop from each sub-plot

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: An essential phenotypic trait estimated using the proposed framework

entities.name: LEAF AREA INDEX (LAI)
entities.description: An essential phenotypic trait estimated using the proposed framework

entities.name: LEAF INCLINATION
entities.description: An essential phenotypic trait estimated using the proposed framework

entities.name: PLANT COUNT
entities.description: An essential phenotypic trait estimated using the proposed framework

entities.name: MUNG BEAN
entities.description: The specific crop of interest used as a use case for the proposed framework

entities.name: COEFFICIENT OF DETERMINATION
entities.description: A metric used to statistically validate the estimates for plant height, LAI, and leaf inclination

entities.name: ROOT MEAN SQUARED ERROR (RMSE)
entities.description: A metric used to statistically validate the estimates for plant height, LAI, and leaf inclination

entities.name: CORRELATION COEFFICIENT
entities.description: A metric used to statistically validate the estimates for plant height, LAI, and leaf inclination

entities.name: REGRESSION ANALYSIS
entities.description: An analytical method performed to gain insights into the data

entities.name: DEEP LEARNING BASED SEGMENTATION METHOD
entities.description: A method explored specifically for the estimation of plant count
